host: i7-11800H (16T) / RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB (GA106, CC 8.6, 30 SM) / Win11 / driver 576.83 / CUDA 12.9 / WDDM

clock_state.i4_pin: DEVIATION — hardware SM-clock pin admin-gated/denied; persistence N/A on WDDM
clock_state.protocol: monitored-boost: interleaved, 25s cooldowns, 250ms throttle log, thermal-bit rejection
clock_state.thermal_note: 92% of thermal events in truncated hot PC3 high-ctx prefills; regression/PC1 ~3% and per-run rejected; SM sags <=8% under thermal (governs C~=0, not bandwidth)

B_bytes_per_decode_step.method: sum of GGUF n_bytes over tensors touched per decode step; tied embeddings => token_embd is LM head read in full

BW_peak_method: device-to-device STREAM triad, median of 3x100 iters (min 264.8, max 272.1), SM 1755 / mem 6000

regression.note: regressed C conflates size-dependent kernel efficiency (MBU rises 0.615->0.648 with size); see direct C

branch.rationale: C~=0 measured directly => Lane A/A1/A2 empty (on-device sampling ships; host submission hidden). 67% BW with C~=0 is kernel-bound. Corroborated by Phase-3 ncu receipt and llama.cpp 0.86 vs 0.67.

PC1_quadratic.note: plateaus at high n; no O(n^2); confirms f32 KV

PC3_context.escalation: decode attention reads f32 KV at ~10-20% of peak BW -> severe under-saturation, Lane K long-context target

escalations.finding: Q4_K_M CUDA decode kernel/dequant-bound (+86% off Q8 line, MBU 0.314 vs 0.615)
escalations.impact: Lane B1/C3 Q4 economics must use measured Q4 TIME not byte ratio

escalations.finding: decode attention reads KV at ~10-20% of peak BW; ~2.7us/ctx-token vs ~0.14us bandwidth cost
escalations.impact: major Lane K long-context target; roofline BW_eff does not apply to KV read
